The **2015 Brisbane Broncos season** was the 28th in [the club](/wiki/Brisbane_Broncos "Brisbane Broncos")'s history. Coached again for the first time since 2008 by returning foundation coach, [Wayne Bennett](/wiki/Wayne_Bennett_(rugby_league) "Wayne Bennett (rugby league)"), and captained by [Justin Hodges](/wiki/Justin_Hodges "Justin Hodges"), they competed in the [NRL](/wiki/National_Rugby_League "National Rugby League")'s [2015 Telstra Premiership](/wiki/2015_NRL_season "2015 NRL season"). The Broncos finished the regular season in 2nd place to make the play-offs, going on to reach the [2015 NRL Grand final](/wiki/2015_NRL_Grand_final "2015 NRL Grand final") in which they were defeated by the [North Queensland Cowboys](/wiki/North_Queensland_Cowboys "North Queensland Cowboys").

#### NRL Auckland Nines

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=2015_Brisbane_Broncos_season&action=edit&section=12 "Edit section: NRL Auckland Nines")]

Main article: [2015 NRL Auckland Nines](/wiki/2015_NRL_Auckland_Nines "2015 NRL Auckland Nines")

The [NRL Auckland Nines](/wiki/NRL_Auckland_Nines "NRL Auckland Nines") is a pre-season [rugby league nines](/wiki/Rugby_league_nines "Rugby league nines") competition featuring all 16 [NRL](/wiki/National_Rugby_League "National Rugby League") clubs. The 2015 competition was played over two days on 31 January and 1 February at [Eden Park](/wiki/Eden_Park "Eden Park"). The Broncos feature in the Piha pool and played the [Bulldogs](/wiki/Canterbury-Bankstown_Bulldogs "Canterbury-Bankstown Bulldogs"), [Sharks](/wiki/Cronulla-Sutherland_Sharks "Cronulla-Sutherland Sharks") and [Roosters](/wiki/Sydney_Roosters "Sydney Roosters").
